file_name: GSITECHNOLOGYINC_11_16_2009-EX-10.2-INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY AGREEMENT between SONY ELECTRONICS INC. and GSI TECHNOLOGY, INC..txt

perturbation.original_text: 1.4 Licensed Patent(s) The term "Licensed Patent" or "Licensed Patents" means any patent or patents issued at any time in any country (including any inventor's certificates, extension, reissued, renewal and reexamined patents), which (1) is based on any invention made on or prior to the Effective Date, including any patents for inventions disclosed in patent applications filed on or prior to the Effective Date, and issuing from any of those patent applications or any patent applications which are continuations, continuing applications, continuations-in-part or divisions of those patent applications, or on any foreign counterpart of any of those patent applications, (2) (a) is owned by SONY or any of its Affiliates or (b) under which SONY or its Affiliates have the right to grant licenses without payment by SONY or any of its Affiliates of additional royalties or other consideration to a third party (excluding payments for inventions made by that third party while employed by SONY or any of its Affiliates), and (3) would, in the absence of a license granted herein, be infringed by making, using, selling, offering for sale, importing or supplying any Competing Products (including, without limitation, the SRAM Products). "Licensed Patents" shall exclude the Transferred Patents.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 Licensed Patent(s) The term "Licensed Patent" or "Licensed Patents" means any patent or patents issued at any time in any country (including any inventor's certificates, extension, reissued, renewal and reexamined patents), which (1) is based on any invention made on or prior to the Effective Date, including any patents for inventions disclosed in patent applications filed on or prior to the Effective Date, and issuing from any of those patent applications or any patent applications which are continuations, continuing applications, continuations-in-part or divisions of those patent applications, or on any foreign counterpart of any of those patent applications, (2) (a) is owned by SONY or any of its Affiliates or (b) under which SONY or its Affiliates have the right to grant licenses without payment by SONY or any of its Affiliates of additional royalties or other consideration to a third party (excluding payments for inventions made by that third party while employed by SONY or any of its Affiliates), and (3) would, in the absence of a license granted herein, be infringed by making, using, selling, offering for sale, importing or supplying any Competing Products. "Licensed Patents" shall exclude the Transferred Patents.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase '(including, without limitation, the SRAM Products)', the definition of 'Licensed Patents' becomes ambiguous. It is no longer explicitly clear whether the license granted under these patents covers SRAM Products. This creates a contradiction with Section 3.1, where SONY grants PURCHASER a license under the Licensed Patents to make, use, sell, and import any Competing Products (including, without limitation, the SRAM Products).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Grant by SONY SONY, on behalf of itself and its Affiliates, hereby grants to PURCHASER a worldwide, non-exclusive, fully paid-up, royalty-free license (a) under the Licensed Patents to make, have made, use, offer to sell, sell, otherwise dispose of, and import any Competing Products (including, without limitation, the SRAM Products); and (b) to use, reproduce, modify, prepare derivative works of, perform, display, and otherwise practice and exploit in any manner any and all of the SRAM Intellectual Property in connection with the use and exploitation of the 3 Transferred Assets, and to make, have made, use, offer to sell, sell, otherwise dispose of, and import Competing Products (including, without limitation, SRAM Products).
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly included 'SRAM Products' within the definition of 'Competing Products' under 'Licensed Patents' (1.4). This ensured consistency with Section 3.1, which grants a license under Licensed Patents to make, use, sell, and import any Competing Products, including SRAM Products. By removing '(including, without limitation, the SRAM Products)' from Section 1.4's definition, the changed text introduces ambiguity. Section 3.1 still explicitly includes SRAM products in the license grant. The changed text in Section 1.4 now contradicts the intent of Section 3.1, making it unclear whether the license covers SRAM products under the Licensed Patents, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty about the obligations and rights.

perturbation.original_text: 1.5 Transferred Patent(s) The term "Transferred Patent" or "Transferred Patents" means the patents and/or patent applications listed in Exhibit A to this IP Agreement and all patents and applications claiming the priority of any of these patents and/or patent applications and all reissues, divisions, renewals, extensions, provisionals, and continuations thereof, and any equivalent or similar rights anywhere in the world in inventions and discoveries.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 Transferred Patent(s) The term "Transferred Patent" or "Transferred Patents" means any patent that the PURCHASER decides after Closing.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of "Transferred Patent", it now gives PURCHASER the liberty to include patents that are not initially intended to be included. This contradicts with what it is written on Section 2, where SONY assigns to PURCHASER the ownership of Transferred Patents and giving PURCHASER the right to claim for damages in future infringements. Making this change, the text will not be clear of how it can be enforced due to the ambiguity of the real meaning of Transferred Patents, which will lead to confusion for the entities involve
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Assignment SONY hereby assigns to PURCHASER SONY's entire right, title and interest in, to and under the Transferred Patents, and any patents that may issue therefrom (including any foreign counterparts, divisions, continuations, renewals, continuations in part, reexaminations or reissues thereof), along with the right to sue and collect damages for any future infringement, and agrees to take all reasonably necessary action to assist PURCHASER, at PURCHASER's sole expense, to register, confirm and perfect such assignment, including by making filings with or at any and all necessary patent offices and/or governmental agencies. SONY retains the right to sue and collect damages for any past infringement of the Transferred Patents, provided that SONY obtains PURCHASER's written consent prior to making any infringement claim or allegation or filing any action, suit, litigation or proceeding that could affect the Transferred Patents or PURCHASER's ability to use and exploit the Transferred Patents or that may result in PURCHASER being joined as a party to the action, suit, litigation or proceeding. PURCHASER shall not unreasonably withhold its consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the definition of "Transferred Patent" to be determined at PURCHASER's discretion after closing, while the original text defines it as specific patents listed in Exhibit A. This contradicts Section 2, which assigns SONY's rights in the specifically defined "Transferred Patents" to PURCHASER. The change introduces ambiguity, making it unclear which patents are actually being assigned, and affects SONY's retained rights regarding past infringement since the scope of "Transferred Patents" is now uncertain. This misalignment in terminology directly creates an in-text contradiction and ambiguity affecting the enforceability of the assignment.

perturbation.original_text: 8.12 Transferability All Transferred Patents will be fully transferable, alienable or licensable by PURCHASER from and after the Closing without restriction and without payment of any kind to any third party other than requirements under applicable laws to file documents with and pay fees to patent offices. There are no Liens on any of the Transferred Patents. (For the avoidance of doubt, the Parties acknowledge that SONY's licensees under Portfolio Cross Licenses already hold licenses to the Transferred Patents and do not need a license from PURCHASER to practice the Transferred Patents.)
perturbation.changed_text: 8.12 Transferability All Transferred Patents will be fully transferable, alienable or licensable by PURCHASER from and after the Closing without restriction and without payment of any kind to any third party other than requirements under applicable laws to file documents with and pay fees to patent offices. There are no Liens on any of the Transferred Patents. (For the avoidance of doubt, the Parties acknowledge that SONY's licensees under Portfolio Cross Licenses already hold licenses to the Assigned Patents and do not need a license from PURCHASER to practice the Transferred Patents.)
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Transferred Patents" to "Assigned Patents" within the parenthetical throws the definition of Assigned Patents up in the air. It also leaves the text within the parentheses in conflict with the remainder of the text. Since Assigned Patents isn't defined it is now an ambiguous term within the document. This is in conflict with Section 2, which states that sony will sign to the PURCHASER the right, title and interest in all Transferred Patents. Now, we also have another term of Assigned Patents that is not defined within the document, and it is hard to know if the term of Transferred Patents also meant Assigned Patents.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Assignment SONY hereby assigns to PURCHASER SONY's entire right, title and interest in, to and under the Transferred Patents, and any patents that may issue therefrom (including any foreign counterparts, divisions, continuations, renewals, continuations in part, reexaminations or reissues thereof), along with the right to sue and collect damages for any future infringement, and agrees to take all reasonably necessary action to assist PURCHASER, at PURCHASER's sole expense, to register, confirm and perfect such assignment, including by making filings with or at any and all necessary patent offices and/or governmental agencies. SONY retains the right to sue and collect damages for any past infringement of the Transferred Patents, provided that SONY obtains PURCHASER's written consent prior to making any infringement claim or allegation or filing any action, suit, litigation or proceeding that could affect the Transferred Patents or PURCHASER's ability to use and exploit the Transferred Patents or that may result in PURCHASER being joined as a party to the action, suit, litigation or proceeding. PURCHASER shall not unreasonably withhold its consent.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Transferred Patents" to "Assigned Patents" in section 8.12 introduces a term, "Assigned Patents," that is not defined in the contract, unlike "Transferred Patents," which is defined in section 2. This creates ambiguity and potential conflict. Section 2 assigns rights, title, and interest in "Transferred Patents," but the modified section 8.12 now refers to "Assigned Patents" in the context of SONY's licensees. Without a clear definition of "Assigned Patents," it's unclear if these patents are the same as or different from the "Transferred Patents," creating a contradiction regarding the scope of the assignment and the rights of SONY's licensees. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations and uncertainty.
